ownership.percentage: 69.02% promoter-group holding after the October 2025 rights issue
ownership.summary: NDTV describes itself as a subsidiary of AMG Media Networks Limited, an Adani Group company. Its 2025 rights-issue statement reported promoter ownership of 69.02%.

ownershipHistory.title: AMG Media Networks announced an indirect acquisition and open offer
ownershipHistory.summary: AMG subsidiary VCPL exercised rights to acquire control of RRPR Holding, which held 29.18% of NDTV, and announced an open offer.

ownershipHistory.title: Promoter holding increased after NDTV's rights issue
ownershipHistory.summary: NDTV reported that promoter-group ownership increased from 64.71% to 69.02% after allotment under the rights issue.

ownership.percentage: Over 82% held by Arnab Goswami in the network's February 2020 disclosure
ownership.summary: Republic currently describes the network as wholly editor-controlled and led by founder Arnab Goswami. Its latest located numerical promoter disclosure, from February 2020, said Goswami held over 82% of ARG Outlier Media.

stake.label: Over 82% held by Arnab Goswami in the latest located numerical disclosure

ownershipHistory.title: Rajeev Chandrasekhar resigned from the Republic TV board
ownershipHistory.summary: Chandrasekhar resigned after becoming a BJP member; reporting at the time said his 16.82% indirect holding through Asianet remained unchanged.

ownershipHistory.title: Asianet reduced its Republic holding through a buyback
ownershipHistory.summary: Asianet reduced its stake as Arnab Goswami bought back shares, leaving Asianet as a minority investor and increasing editor control.

ownershipHistory.title: Republic disclosed Arnab Goswami's controlling stake
ownershipHistory.summary: The network said Goswami held more than 82% of ARG Outlier Media and was its sole promoter and board chairman.

ownership.parentCompany: Independent Media Trust and other promoter-group entities
ownership.percentage: 56.89% held by Reliance Industries through Independent Media Trust and other entities
ownership.summary: Network18's current corporate disclosure says Reliance Industries holds 56.89% of the company through Independent Media Trust and other entities. Reliance is the sole beneficiary of Independent Media Trust.

stake.label: 56.89% held by Reliance Industries through Independent Media Trust and other entities

ownershipHistory.title: Independent Media Trust acquired control of Network18
ownershipHistory.summary: Independent Media Trust, of which Reliance Industries is the sole beneficiary, completed its acquisition of control of Network18 and TV18.

ownershipHistory.title: TV18 and e-Eighteen merged into Network18
ownershipHistory.summary: The court-approved merger became effective, bringing Network18's television news and digital news businesses into one listed entity.

ownership.percentage: 51% Prakash family and 49% Reuters in the latest public shareholding statement located, filed in 2020 litigation
ownership.summary: ANI is operated by ANI Media Private Limited. The latest public shareholding statement located is in 2020 court proceedings and describes the Prakash family as holding 51% and Reuters as holding 49%; current corporate records list Sanjiv Prakash as managing director.

stake.label: 51% Prakash family and 49% Reuters in the latest public shareholding statement located

ownershipHistory.title: Prem Prakash incorporated the company that became ANI Media
ownershipHistory.summary: The company was incorporated as Asian Films Laboratories Private Limited and renamed ANI Media Private Limited in 1997.

ownershipHistory.title: Reuters acquired 49% of the company
ownershipHistory.summary: The Prakash family retained 51% while Reuters acquired 49%, according to the share purchase and shareholder arrangements recorded by the Supreme Court.

ownership.operatingCompany: The Press Trust of India Limited
ownership.ultimateParent: Board-governed news agency with directors drawn largely from Indian newspaper publishers
ownership.percentage: No single controlling percentage disclosed on PTI's current public pages
ownership.summary: PTI publishes its board rather than identifying a single controlling owner. The current board is composed largely of leaders from major Indian newspaper groups, alongside independent directors.

stake.label: No single controlling percentage is disclosed on PTI's current public pages

ownershipHistory.title: Press Trust of India was registered
ownershipHistory.summary: PTI says it was registered in 1947 and began operating in 1949.

name: IANS India Private Limited

ownership.percentage: Wholly owned by AMG Media Networks following completion of the balance-stake acquisition in March 2026
ownership.summary: IANS is a wholly owned subsidiary of AMG Media Networks, the media subsidiary of Adani Enterprises, following completion of the remaining-share acquisition in March 2026.

stake.label: Wholly owned by AMG Media Networks

businessInterests: Media consultancy, software and mobile services

ownershipHistory.title: AMG Media Networks acquired a majority stake in IANS
ownershipHistory.summary: AMG Media Networks acquired 50.50% of IANS, making it a subsidiary.

ownershipHistory.title: AMG Media Networks completed its acquisition of IANS
ownershipHistory.summary: Adani Enterprises announced completion of the balance-stake acquisition, making IANS a wholly owned step-down subsidiary.

name: United News of India (UNI)

ownership.percentage: The Statesman acquired 100% after its insolvency resolution plan was approved and implemented in 2025
ownership.summary: UNI was historically owned by a group of newspaper shareholders. That structure changed after insolvency proceedings: The Statesman's resolution plan was approved in February 2025 and the takeover was subsequently implemented.

stake.label: 100% acquired by The Statesman after the approved insolvency resolution

businessInterests: English, Hindi, Urdu and Kannada newswire services

ownershipHistory.title: UNI remained owned by multiple newspaper shareholders
ownershipHistory.summary: UNI's published 2022 accounts listed ABP, The Statesman, Express Publications, HT Media, Bennett Coleman and other newspaper interests among its principal shareholders.

ownershipHistory.title: NCLT approved The Statesman's resolution plan
ownershipHistory.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal approved The Statesman's plan for UNI; the approval was later upheld on appeal.

ownershipHistory.title: The Statesman completed its takeover of UNI
ownershipHistory.summary: The Statesman acquired 100% of UNI after the insolvency resolution, replacing the previous multi-newspaper shareholder structure.
